name.en: Khao Sam Roi Yot National Park
name.nl: Khao Sam Roi Yot Nationaal Park

address: Khao Sam Roi Yot, Kui Buri, Prachuap Khiri Khan 77150
entry_fee: 200 THB foreigners, 100 THB children; Thai nationals 40 THB adults, 20 THB children. Boat to Phraya Nakhon Cave trailhead approx 400 THB per boat
visit_duration: Half day (4–6 hours) for Phraya Nakhon Cave, full day for the complete park
best_time_to_visit: Arrive by 09:00 to reach the cave before 10:30 when sunlight illuminates the royal pavilion

description.en:
## A Coastal National Park with Thailand's Most Iconic Cave

Khao Sam Roi Yot, meaning 'Mountain of Three Hundred Peaks', is a 98-square-kilometre national park about 60 kilometres south of Hua Hin. Its dramatic landscape of jagged limestone karsts rising from coastal marshland and mangrove forests makes it one of the most visually stunning parks in central Thailand. The headline attraction is Phraya Nakhon Cave, but the park offers much more for nature lovers willing to explore.

## Phraya Nakhon Cave

This massive sinkhole cave is home to Phra Thinang Khuha Kharuhat, a four-gabled royal pavilion built in 1890 for King Rama V. Between 10:30 and 11:30 in the morning, a shaft of natural sunlight streams through the collapsed ceiling and illuminates the golden pavilion — creating one of the most photographed scenes in all of Thailand. The hike to the cave covers about 2 kilometres over moderate terrain with limestone rocks, taking 60 to 90 minutes each way.

## Mangroves and Wildlife

The park's coastal wetlands include a boardwalk trail through mangrove forests where you can spot mudskippers, fiddler crabs, and wading birds. Khao Sam Roi Yot is home to over 300 bird species, making it a premier birdwatching destination. The freshwater marshes are the largest remaining wetlands on the central Thai coast.

## Other Highlights

Sam Phraya Beach is a secluded bay accessible by boat, ideal for swimming and picnics. Kaeo Cave, another smaller cavern, features impressive stalactite formations and is much less crowded than Phraya Nakhon.

## Getting There

From Hua Hin, drive south on Highway 4 for about an hour. Alternatively, join a guided day tour from Hua Hin starting at around 1,500 THB per person including transport and lunch.
description.nl: Kust nationaal park met de beroemde Phraya Nakhon-grot, mangroven en kalksteenpieken

tips: Start hiking by 09:00 to reach the cave before 10:30 when the sunbeam lights up the pavilion
Wear sturdy hiking shoes — the limestone trail is uneven and slippery, especially after rain
Bring at least 1.5 litres of water per person as there are no shops along the trail
